title: Atom Probe Tomography of Molecular Organic Materials: Sub-Dalton Nanometer-Scale Quantiﬁcation
authors: Proudian, Andrew P.; Jaskot, Matthew B.; Diercks, David R.; Gorman, Brian P.; Zimmerman, Jeramy D.
abstract: In this paper, we demonstrate that atom probe tomography (APT) can be applied to small-molecule organic materials. We show that APT can provide an unprecedented combination of mass resolution of <1 Da, spatial resolution of ∼0.3 nm in z and ∼1 nm in x−y, and analytic sensitivity of ∼50 ppm with no evidence of molecular fragmentation.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, Molecular Organic Materials, Sub-Dalton Nanometer-Scale Quantiﬁcation

title: Atom Probe Tomography Unveils Formation Mechanisms of Wear-Protective Triboﬁlms by ZDDP, Ionic Liquid, and Their Combination
authors: Guo, Wei; Zhou, Yan; Sang, Xiahan; Leonard, Donovan N.; Qu, Jun; Poplawsky, Jonathan D.
abstract: In this work, APT and STEM were used to investigate the tribofilms formed by three different lubricant antiwear additives, a phosphonium-phosphate IL, ZDDP, and a combination of both IL+ZDDP.
keywords: triboﬁlm, atom probe tomography, ionic liquid, ZDDP, lubricant, STEM-EELS, APT, STEM, tribofilms, lubricant additives, ionic liquids

title: Atomic-Scale Characterization of N-Doped Si Nanocrystals Embedded in SiO2 by Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Demoulin, R.; Roussel, M.; Duguay, S.; Muller, D.; Mathiot, D.; Pareige, P.; Talbot, E.
journal: The Journal of Physical Chemistry C
abstract: Structural properties of undoped, As-doped, and P-doped silicon nanocrystals (Si-Ncs) embedded in a SiO2 matrix have been investigated using atom probe tomography.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, Silicon Nanocrystals, Doping, SiO2, Phosphorus Concentration, Silicon Nanostructures

title: Probing catalytic surfaces by correlative scanning photoemission electron microscopy and atom probe tomography
authors: Schweinar, Kevin; Nicholls, Rachel L.; Rajamathi, Catherine R.; Zeller, Patrick; Amati, Matteo; Gregoratti, Luca; Raabe, Dierk; Greiner, Mark; Gault, Baptiste; Kasian, Olga
abstract: In this work we presented a correlative microscopy approach to investigate catalytic surfaces with a high surface sensitivity and at the near-atomic scale. The developed experimental strategy involves scanning photoemission electron microscopy (SPEM) and atom probe tomography (APT) applied on the identical location on a sample surface. This involves a sample design developed to suit electrochemical or thermal oxidation treatments (or similar), and all involved consecutive experimental steps.
keywords: Atom probe tomography, Scanning photoemission electron microscopy, Correlative microscopy, Catalytic surfaces, Mixed oxides, Near-atomic scale

title: Challenging Prevalent Solid Electrolyte Interphase (SEI) Models: An Atom Probe Tomography Study on a Commercial Graphite Electrode
authors: Pantenburg, Isabel; Cronau, Marvin; Boll, Torben; Duncker, Annalena; Roling, Bernhard
abstract: Lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) are the dominating energy storage technology for electric vehicles and portable electronic devices. Since the resources of raw materials for LIBs are limited and recycling technologies for LIBs are still under development, improvements in the long-term stability of LIBs are of paramount importance and, in addition, would lead to a reduction in the levelized cost of storage (LCOS). A crucial limiting factor is the aging of the solid electrolyte interphase (SEI) on the active material particles in the anode.
keywords: lithium-ion batteries, energy storage, solid electrolyte interphase, atom probe tomography, nanostructure

title: Characterization of Oxidation and Reduction of a Palladium−Rhodium Alloy by Atom-Probe Tomography
authors: Li, Tong; Bagot, Paul A. J.; Marquis, Emmanuelle A.; Tsang, S. C. Edman; Smith, George D. W.
journal: The Journal of Physical Chemistry C
abstract: Platinum group metals (PGMs) are used in numerous catalyst applications, including conversion of engine exhaust gases and hydrocarbon reforming. Reducing the loading of PGMs without diminishing the overall catalyst activity is a major challenge.
keywords: Atom-Probe Tomography, Palladium-Rhodium Alloy, Oxidation, Reduction

title: Dopant Distribution in Atomic Layer Deposited ZnO:Al Films Visualized by Transmission Electron Microscopy and Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Wu, Yizhi; Giddings, A. Devin; Verheijen, Marcel A.; Macco, Bart; Prosa, Ty J.; Larson, David J.; Roozeboom, Fred; Kessels, Wilhelmus M. M.
abstract: The maximum conductivity achievable in Al-doped ZnO thin films prepared by atomic layer deposition (ALD) is limited by the low doping efficiency of Al. To better understand the limiting factors for the doping efficiency, the three-dimensional distribution of Al atoms in the ZnO host material matrix has been examined on the atomic scale using a combination of high-resolution transmission electron microscopy (TEM) and atom probe tomography (APT).
keywords: Al-doped ZnO, atomic layer deposition, atom probe tomography, transmission electron microscopy

title: Composition Metrology of Ternary Semiconductor Alloys Analyzed by Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Di Russo, E.; Moyon, F.; Gogneau, N.; Largeau, L.; Giraud, E.; Carlin, J.-F.; Grandjean, N.; Chauveau, J. M.; Hugues, M.; Blum, I.; Lefebvre, W.; Vurpillot, F.; Blavette, D.; Rigutti, L.
journal: The Journal of Physical Chemistry C
abstract: Ternary semiconductor alloys based on the AyB1−yC stoichiometry are widely employed in electronic devices, and their composition plays a key role in band gap engineering of heterostructures.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, Ternary Semiconductor Alloys, Composition Metrology, Wide Bandgap Semiconductors, Nanostructures, Surface Electric Field

title: Nonlinear elastic aspects of multi-component iron oxide core–shell nanowires by means of atom probe tomography, analytical microscopy, and nonlinear mechanics
authors: Csiszár, Gábor; Solodenko, Helena; Lawitzki, Robert; Ma, Wenhao; Everett, Christopher; Csiszár, Orsolya
abstract: One-dimensional nanowires (NWs) have gained considerable attention in the last decades due to their outstanding optical, electrical, magnetic, chemical and mechanical properties in order to be used as building blocks in novel nanotechnological applications such as photocatalysis, energy conversion applications, micro/nano electromechanical systems and sensing.
keywords: Atom probe tomography, nanowires, iron oxide, nonlinear mechanics, Elastic Deformation, Oxide Shell

title: Visualizing local fast ionic conduction pathways in nanocrystalline lanthanum manganite by isotope exchange-atom probe tomography
authors: Chiabrera, Francesco; Baiutti, Federico; Diercks, David; Cavallaro, Andrea; Aguadero, Ainara; Morata, Alex; Tarancón, Albert
abstract: The modiﬁcation of the local chemistry via the introduction of an interface is a strategy with great potential for engineering the electrochemical properties of functional oxides beyond the state of the art.
keywords: Atom probe tomography, Isotope exchange, Lanthanum manganite, Grain boundaries, Oxygen conduction

title: Understanding the effects of fabrication process on BaZr0.9Y0.1O3-δ grain-boundary chemistry using atom probe tomography
authors: Clark, Daniel R.; Diercks, David R.; Ricote, Sandrine; Tauer Dearden, Tania; Sullivan, Neal P.; Medlin, J. Will; Gormana, Brian P.; O'Hayre, Ryan P.
abstract: Proton-conducting oxides, such as BaZr0.9Y0.1O3-δ (BZY10), are receiving significant scientific attention for application in a variety of electrochemical applications.
keywords: Atom probe tomography, BaZr0.9Y0.1O3-δ, grain-boundary chemistry, proton-conducting oxides

title: Detecting Cage Crossing and Filling Clusters of Magnesium and Carbon Atoms in Zeolite SSZ-13 with Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Van Vreeswijk, Sophie H.; Monai, Matteo; Oord, Ramon; Schmidt, Joel E.; Parvulescu, Andrei N.; Yarulina, Irina; Karwacki, Lukasz; Poplawsky, Jonathan D.; Weckhuysen, Bert M.
abstract: The conversion of methanol to valuable hydrocarbon molecules is of great commercial interest, as the process serves as a sustainable alternative for the production of, for instance, the base chemicals for plastics. The reaction is catalyzed by zeolite materials. By the introduction of magnesium as a cationic metal, the properties of the zeolite, and thereby the catalytic performance, are changed. With atom probe tomography (APT), nanoscale relations within zeolite materials can be revealed: i.e., crucial information for a fundamental mechanistic understanding.
keywords: Zeolites, Methanol-to-Hydrocarbons, Atom Probe Tomography, Operando UV−Vis Spectroscopy, Operando X-ray Diffraction, Zeolite SSZ-13, Magnesium Modification, Coke Formation, Methanol-to-Hydrocarbons Reaction

title: Effects of isoconcentration surface threshold values on the characteristics of needle-shaped precipitates in atom probe tomography data from an aged Al–Mg–Si alloy
authors: Aruga, Yasuhiro; Kozuka, Masaya
abstract: Needle-shaped precipitates in an aged Al–0.62Mg–0.93Si (mass%) alloy were identified using a compositional threshold method, an isoconcentration surface, in atom probe tomography (APT). The influence of thresholds on the morphological and compositional characteristics of the precipitates was investigated.
keywords: aluminum alloys, atom probe tomography, precipitate, isoconcentration surface, composition profile, transmission electron microscopy

title: Radiation-induced segregation in W-Re: from kinetic Monte Carlo simulations to atom probe tomography experiments
authors: Lloyd, Matthew J.; Abernethy, Robert G.; Armstrong, David E.J.; Bagot, Paul A.J.; Moody, Michael P.; Martinez, Enrique; Nguyen-Manh, Duc
abstract: A viable fusion power station is reliant on the development of plasma facing materials that can withstand the combined effects of high temperature operation and high neutron doses. In this study we focus on W, the most promising candidate material. Re is the primary transmutation product and has been shown to induce embrittlement through cluster formation and precipitation below its predicted solubility limit in W.
keywords: W-Re, radiation-induced segregation, kinetic Monte Carlo simulations, atom probe tomography, Ion irradiation, Grain boundary segregation, Re precipitation

title: Exploring the Surface Segregation of Rh Dopants in PtNi Nanoparticles through Atom Probe Tomography Analysis
authors: Kim, Se-Ho; Jun, Hosun; Jang, Kyuseon; Choi, Pyuck-Pa; Gault, Baptiste; Jung, Chanwon
journal: The Journal of Physical Chemistry C
abstract: Proton-exchange membrane fuel cells hold promise as energy conversion devices for hydrogen-based power generation and storage.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, PtNi nanoparticles, Rh dopants, surface segregation

title: Extreme Ultraviolet Radiation Pulsed Atom Probe Tomography of III-Nitride Semiconductor Materials
authors: Miaja-Avila, Luis; Caplins, Benjamin W.; Chiaramonti, Ann N.; Blanchard, Paul T.; Brubaker, Matt D.; Davydov, Albert V.; Diercks, David R.; Gorman, Brian P.; Rishinaramangalam, Ashwin; Feezell, Daniel F.; Bertness, Kris A.; Sanford, Norman A.
journal: The Journal of Physical Chemistry C
abstract: Laser-pulsed atom probe tomography (LAPT) is a materials characterization technique that has been widely applied in the study and characterization of III-nitride semiconductors. To date, most of these studies have used light sources ranging from the visible to the near-ultraviolet region of the spectrum. In this manuscript, we demonstrate that a recently developed extreme ultraviolet (EUV) radiation pulsed atom probe can trigger controlled field ion evaporation from III-nitride samples.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, III-Nitride Semiconductors, Extreme Ultraviolet Radiation, EUV light

title: Cryo-focused ion beam preparation of perovskite-based solar cells for atom probe tomography
authors: Rivas, Nicolas Alfonso; Babayigit, Aslihan; Conings, Bert; Schwarz, Torsten; Sturm, Andreas; Garzon Manjon, Alba; Cojocaru-Miretin, Oana; Gault, Baptiste; Renner, Frank Uwe
abstract: Focused-ion beam lift-out and annular milling is the most common method used for obtaining site specific specimens for atom probe tomography (APT) experiments and transmission electron microscopy. However, one of the main limitations of this technique comes from the structural damage as well as chemical degradation caused by the beam of high-energy ions.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, Perovskite Solar Cells, Cryo-Focused Ion Beam

title: Nanoscale heterogeneity of U and Pb in baddeleyite from atom probe tomography – 238U series alpha recoil effects and U atom clustering
authors: Denyszyn, Steven; Davis, Donald W.; Fougerouse, Denis
abstract: Atom probe tomography (APT) of 238U and 206Pb has been applied to baddeleyite crystals from the Hart Dolerite (1791 ± 1 Ma) and the Great Dyke of Mauritania (2732±2 Ma) in an effort to constrain the average nuclear recoil distance of 238U series daughter nuclei and correct alpha-recoil-induced Pb loss on U–Pb ages from small baddeleyite crystals.
keywords: baddeleyite, atom probe tomography, U-Pb geochronology, alpha recoil, nanoscale heterogeneity, uranium, lead

title: Interpreting the Presence of an Additional Oxide Layer in Analysis of Metal Oxides−Metal Interfaces in Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Bachhav, Mukesh; Pawar, Gorakh; Vurpillot, Francois; Danoix, Raphaele; Danoix, Frederic; Hannoyer, Beatrice; Dong, Yan; Marquis, Emmanuelle
journal: The Journal of Physical Chemistry C
abstract: Atom Probe Tomography (APT) analysis of specimens embedded with metal oxide/metal leads to nonintuitive observations of a very thin layer of oxide at the interface due to oxygen migration under the influence of electric field in metal oxides.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, Metal Oxides, Metal Interfaces, Oxygen Migration, Electric Field

title: Elucidating the Structure and Composition of Individual Bimetallic Nanoparticles in Supported Catalysts by Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Zand, Florian; Hangx, Suzanne J. T.; Spiers, Christopher J.; Van Den Brink, Peter J.; Burns, James; Boebinger, Matthew G.; Poplawsky, Jonathan D.; Monai, Matteo; Weckhuysen, Bert M.
journal: Journal of the American Chemical Society
abstract: Understanding and controlling the structure and composition of nanoparticles in supported metal catalysts are crucial to improve chemical processes. For this, atom probe tomography (APT) is a unique tool, as it allows for spatially resolved three-dimensional chemical imaging of materials with sub-nanometer resolution.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, Bimetallic Nanoparticles, Supported Catalysts, CO2 Hydrogenation, nickel, structure sensitivity

title: Isolating Clusters of Light Elements in Molecular Sieves with Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Schmidt, Joel E.; Peng, Linqing; Lucini Paioni, Alessandra; Ehren, Helena Leona; Guo, Wei; Mazumder, Baishakhi; De Winter, D. A. Matthijs; Attila, Özgün; Fu, Donglong; Dutta Chowdhury, Abhishek; Houben, Klaartje; Baldus, Marc; Poplawsky, Jonathan D.; Weckhuysen, Bert M.
journal: Journal of the American Chemical Society
abstract: Understanding the 3-D distribution and nature of active sites in heterogeneous catalysts is critical to developing structure−function relationships.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, Molecular Sieves, Catalysts, Heterogeneous Catalysts, Active Sites

title: An atom probe tomography investigation of Ti–MoS2 and MoS2–Sb2O3–Au films
authors: Singh, Harpal; Mutyala, Kalyan C.; Evans, Ryan D.; Doll, Gary L.

title: Interfaces and defect composition at the near-atomic scale through atom probe tomography investigations
authors: Gault, Baptiste; Breen, Andrew J.; Chang, Yanhong; He, Junyang; Jägle, Eric A.; Kontis, Paraskevas; Kürnsteiner, Philipp; Kwiatkowski Da Silva, Alisson; Makineni, Surendra Kumar; Mouton, Isabelle; Peng, Zirong; Ponge, Dirk; Schwarz, Torsten; Stephenson, Leigh T.; Szczepaniak, Agnieszka; Zhao, Huan; Raabe, Dierk
abstract: Atom probe tomography (APT) is rising in influence across many parts of materials science and engineering thanks to its unique combination of highly sensitive composition measurement and three-dimensional microstructural characterization.
keywords: Atom probe tomography, Interfaces, Defects, Composition, Materials science

title: APAV: An Open-Source Python Package for Mass Spectrum Analysis in Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Smith, Jesse D.; Young, Marcus L.
abstract: As microstructures are increasingly engineered with nanoscale precision, comparably precise metrological tools like Atom Probe Tomography (APT) are crucial for experimental validation.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, Mass Spectrum Analysis, Open-Source Python Package

title: Quantitative Analysis of Nitrogen by Atom Probe Tomography Using Stoichiometric γ′-Fe4N Consisting of 15N Isotope
authors: Takahashi, Jun; Kawakami, Kazuto; Miura, Koyo; Hirano, Mitsuhiro; Ohtsu, Naofumi
abstract: The nitrogen deficiency in steels measured by atom probe tomography (APT) is considered to arise from the obscurement of singly charged dimer nitrogen ions (N2+) by the iron-dominant peak (56Fe2+) at 28 Da. To verify this by quantifying the amount of N2+ ions, γ′-Fe4N consisting of the 15N isotope was prepared on iron substrates by plasma nitriding using a nitrogen isotopic gas (15N2).
keywords: atom probe, γ′-Fe4N, isotope effect, nitrogen deficiency, nitrogen isotope

title: Correlation of Multiplicity and Chemistry in AlxGa1−xN Heterostructure via Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Licata, Olivia G.; Broderick, Scott R.; Mazumder, Baishakhi
abstract: In this work, the correlation between composition and relative evaporation field was investigated by tracking the statistics of multi-hit detector events in atom probe tomography (APT). This approach is applied systematically to a GaN-based nitride heterostructure with five AlxGa1−xN layers of varying Al composition.
keywords: atom probe tomography, field evaporation, heterostructure, multiplicity, semiconductor

title: Spatial Resolution in Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Gault, Baptiste; Moody, Michael P.; De Geuser, Frederic; La Fontaine, Alex; Stephenson, Leigh T.; Haley, Daniel; Ringer, Simon P.
abstract: In this study, we define a new standard for the assessment of the resolution in atom probe tomography. Methods to quantify both the in-depth and lateral resolutions have been proposed based on the use of advanced spatial distribution maps available to most atom probe users.
keywords: atom probe tomography, resolution, tomographic reconstruction, spatial distribution maps, trajectory aberration, Spatial Resolution, Lateral Resolution, In-Depth Resolution

title: Study on Quantitative Analysis of Carbon and Nitrogen in Stoichiometric θ-Fe3C and γ′-Fe4N by Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Takahashi, Jun; Kawakami, Kazuto; Kobayashi, Yukiko
abstract: The quantitative analysis performance of carbon and nitrogen was investigated using stoichiometric θ-Fe3C (25 at% C) and γ′-Fe4N (∼20 at% N) precipitates in pulsed voltage and pulsed laser atom probes.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, Carbon, Nitrogen, Stoichiometric θ-Fe3C, γ′-Fe4N

title: Reflections on the Projection of Ions in Atom Probe Tomography
authors: De Geuser, Frédéric; Gault, Baptiste
abstract: There are two main projections used to transform, and reconstruct, field ion micrographs or atom probe tomography data into atomic coordinates at the specimen surface and, subsequently, in three dimensions.
keywords: atom probe tomography, field ion microscopy, tomographic reconstruction, projection

title: Exploring New Science Domains with Atom Probe Tomography Enabled by an Environmental Transfer Hub
authors: Perea, Daniel E.; Schreiber, Daniel K.; Devaraj, Arun; Reilly, Dallas D.; Lambeets, Sten V.; Kautz, Elizabeth J.; Lach, Timothy G.; Wirth, Mark G.; Evans, James E.
abstract: The liquid/solid and gas/solid interface represents a rich scientific and technological frontier for discovery and innovation in many areas of science. However, the direct nano- to atomic-scale characterization of these interfaces is technically challenging within broad range of analytical tools that require high to ultrahigh vacuum conditions.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, Environmental Transfer Hub, Liquid/Solid Interface, Gas/Solid Interface

title: Atom Probe Tomography Interlaboratory Study on Clustering Analysis in Experimental Data Using the Maximum Separation Distance Approach
authors: Dong, Yan; Etienne, Auriane; Frolov, Alex; Fedotova, Svetlana; Fujii, Katsuhiko; Fukuya, Koji; Hatzoglou, Constantinos; Kuleshova, Evgenia; Lindgren, Kristina; London, Andrew; Lopez, Anabelle; Lozano-Perez, Sergio; Miyahara, Yuichi; Nagai, Yasuyoshi; Nishida, Kenji; Radiguet, Bertrand; Schreiber, Daniel K.; Soneda, Naoki; Thuvander, Mattias; Toyama, Takeshi; Wang, Jing; Sefta, Faiza; Chou, Peter; Marquis, Emmanuelle A.
abstract: We summarize the findings from an interlaboratory study conducted between ten international research groups and investigate the use of the commonly used maximum separation distance and local concentration thresholding methods for solute clustering quantification.
keywords: atom probe tomography, cluster analysis, maximum separation, Solute Clustering, Interlaboratory Study

title: Enhancing Element Identification by Expectation–Maximization Method in Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Vurpillot, Francois; Hatzoglou, Constantinos; Radiguet, Bertrand; Da Costa, Gerald; Delaroche, Fabien; Danoix, Frederic
abstract: This paper describes an alternative way to assign elemental identity to atoms collected by atom probe tomography (APT). This method is based on Bayesian assignation of label through the expectation–maximization method (well known in data analysis). Assuming the correct shape of mass over charge peaks in mass spectra, the probability of each atom to be labeled as a given element is determined, and is used to enhance data visualization and composition mapping in APT analyses.
keywords: atom probe tomography, data mining, general mixture models, mass spectrum

title: Interface Segregation and Nitrogen Measurement in Fe–Mn–N Steel by Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Langelier, Brian; Van Landeghem, Hugo P.; Botton, Gianluigi A.; Zurob, Hatem S.
abstract: Improved understanding of the interactions between solutes and the austenite/ferrite interface can benefit modeling of ferrite growth during austenite decomposition, as the transformation kinetic is significantly affected by solutes that influence interface mobility.
keywords: atom probe tomography, interface segregation, nitrogen quantification, martensite, steel

title: Fabrication of Atom Probe Tomography Specimens from Nanoparticles Using a Fusible Bi–In–Sn Alloy as an Embedding Medium
authors: Kim, Se-Ho; Lee, Ji Yeong; Ahn, Jae-Pyoung; Choi, Pyuck-Pa
abstract: We propose a new method for preparing atom probe tomography specimens from nanoparticles using a fusible bismuth–indium–tin alloy as an embedding medium.
keywords: atom probe tomography, fusible alloy, nanoparticles

title: Analyzing Boron in 9–12% Chromium Steels Using Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Fedorova, Irina; Grumsen, Flemming Bjerg; Hald, John; Andrén, Hans-Olof; Liu, Fang
abstract: Small additions of boron can remarkably improve the long-term creep resistance of 9–12% Cr steels. The improvement has been attributed to boron segregation to grain boundaries during quenching, and subsequent boron incorporation into certain families of precipitates during tempering.
keywords: borides, carbides, custom-made specimen holder, equilibrium segregation, non-equilibrium segregation, precipitates, prior austenite grain boundaries, quantification, TKD

title: Atom Probe Tomography Productivity Enhancements
authors: Reinhard, D.A.; Payne, T.R.; Strennen, E.M.; Oltman, E.; Geiser, B.P.; Sobering, G.S.; Lenz, D.R.; Mandt, J.; Groth, G.A.; Larson, D.J.; Ulfig, R.M.; Prosa, T.J.
abstract: The semiconductor industry is driven by the minimization of feature size and optimization of process time (money). The combination of these concepts is well captured in the recent literature by the statement that expectations for data are “as precise as possible” and “as fast as possible” [1]. While the atom probe tomography (APT) technique has been around for 50 years [2], only recently has there been substantial efforts toward application in the semiconductor industry (for some recent reviews see [3–5]). The interest in APT is due to its combination of analytical sensitivity and sub-nanometer spatial resolution.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, Productivity Enhancements, Semiconductor Industry

title: Analysis of Natural Rutile (TiO2) by Laser-assisted Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Verberne, Rick; Saxey, David W.; Reddy, Steven M.; Rickard, William D. A.; Fougerouse, Denis; Clark, Chris
abstract: Since the introduction of laser-assisted atom probe, analysis of nonconductive materials by atom probe tomography (APT) has become more routine.
keywords: atom probe tomography, data quality, geoanalytical techniques, rutile/titania, TiO2

title: Novel Applications of FIB-SEM-Based ToF-SIMS in Atom Probe Tomography Workflows
authors: Rickard, William D. A.; Reddy, Steven M.; Saxey, David W.; Fougerouse, Denis; Timms, Nicholas E.; Daly, Luke; Peterman, Emily; Cavosie, Aaron J.; Jourdan, Fred
abstract: Atom probe tomography (APT) is used to quantify atomic-scale elemental and isotopic compositional variations within a very small volume of material (typically <0.01 µm3).
keywords: FIB-SEM, atom probe tomography, sample preparation, FIB-ToF-SIMS

title: Revisiting Temporal Evolution of Cu-Rich Precipitates in Fe–Cu Alloy: Correlative Small Angle Neutron Scattering and Atom-Probe Tomography Studies
authors: Ahlawat, Sarita; Sarkar, Sudip Kumar; Sen, Debasis; Biswas, Aniruddha
abstract: Binary Fe–Cu alloys are effective prototypes for investigating radiation-induced formation and growth of nanometric Cu-rich precipitates (CRPs) in nuclear reactor pressure vessels.

authors: Amouyal, Yaron; Seidman, David N.
abstract: Recent developments in the technology of laser-pulsed local-electrode atom-probe LEAP! tomography include a picosecond ultraviolet UV! laser system having a 355 nm wavelength and both external and in-vacuum optics.
keywords: atom probe tomography, green-laser pulsing, ultraviolet laser pulsing, nickel-based superalloys, signal-to-noise ratio, mass-resolving power, None

title: A Liquid Metal Encapsulation for Analyzing Porous Nanomaterials by Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Kim, Se-Ho; El-Zoka, Ayman A.; Gault, Baptiste
abstract: Analyzing porous (nano)materials via atom probe tomography has been notoriously difficult.
keywords: atom probe tomography, fusible alloy, liquid metal, porous iron

title: Correlative Energy-Dispersive X-Ray Spectroscopic Tomography and Atom Probe Tomography of the Phase Separation in an Alnico 8 Alloy
authors: Guo, Wei; Sneed, Brian T.; Zhou, Lin; Tang, Wei; Kramer, Matthew J.; Cullen, David A.; Poplawsky, Jonathan D.
abstract: Alnico alloys have long been used as strong permanent magnets because of their ferromagnetism and high coercivity. Understanding their structural details allows for better prediction of the resulting magnetic properties. However, quantitative three-dimensional characterization of the phase separation in these alloys is still challenged by the spatial quantiﬁcation of nanoscale phases.
keywords: phase separation, atom probe tomography (APT), electron tomography, correlative tomography, alnico alloy

title: Frozen n-Tetradecane Investigated by Cryo-Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Meng, Kuan; Schwarz, Tim M.; Weikum, Eric M.; Stender, Patrick; Schmitz, Guido
abstract: Atom probe tomography (APT) is a powerful tool for the analysis of the chemical composition of materials at the nanoscale. In this study, we demonstrate the use of APT to map the chemical composition of mammalian cells. We show that APT can be used to identify the presence of specific elements and compounds within cells, and to map their distribution at the nanoscale. Our results demonstrate the potential of APT as a tool for the analysis of the chemical composition of cells, and highlight the need for further research in this area.
keywords: Atom probe tomography, frozen liquid, n-tetradecane, cryo-FIB, cryo-APT, Chemical mapping, Mammalian cells

title: Developing Atom Probe Tomography to Characterize Sr-Loaded Bioactive Glass for Bone Scaffolding
authors: Ren, Yanru; Autefage, Hélène; Jones, Julian R.; Stevens, Molly M.; Bagot, Paul A.J.; Moody, Michael P.
abstract: In this study, atom probe tomography (APT) was used to investigate strontium-containing bioactive glass particles (BG-Sr10) and strontium-releasing bioactive glass-based scaffolds (pSrBG), both of which are attractive biomaterials with applications in critical bone damage repair.
keywords: atom probe tomography, bioactive glass, parameter optimization, strontium-releasing materials, Focused ion beam, Ultramicrotome, Sample preparation, Polymer electrolyte membrane fuel cell, Analytical microscopies

title: Probing the Miscibility Gap of the Pt–Pd Binary System by Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Lee, Yoonhee; Stender, Patrick; Eich, Sebastian Manuel; Schmitz, Guido
abstract: To solve the uncertainty of the platinum (Pt)–palladium (Pd) phase diagram, especially the existence of a suggested miscibility gap, atom probe tomography (APT) was used to determine the time evolution of the composition after heat treatment.
keywords: atom probe tomography, diffusion, miscibility gap, mixing tendency, Pt–Pd phase diagram

title: Directions in Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Larson, David; Lenz, Dan; Martin, Isabelle; Prosa, Ty; Reinhard, David; Clifton, Peter; Geiser, Brian; Ulfig, Robert; Bunton, Joe
abstract: Peer reviewed manuscripts that include research using atom probe tomography (APT) [1] have increased at an amazing pace (~5X) since about 2005 [2]. Such research relies on continued advances in the areas of hardware and software related to APT. The following topics:  laser wavelength [3], advanced reconstruction algorithms [4], automated data acquisition and analysis [5], cryo/vacuum transfer [6], mass resolving power (MRP) – field of view tradeoffs (FOV) [7-9], and multi-direction laser incidence [10] are all areas in which CAMECA is working in order to promote enhanced APT data quality.  The latter two are the focus of the current abstract.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, APT, Directions, Advances, Hardware, Software

title: Electrostatic Reconstruction Technology in Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Geiser, Brian; Martin, Isabelle; Reinhard, David; Lenz, Dan; Prosa, Ty; Ulfig, Robert; Larson, David
abstract: In atom probe tomography (APT) a primary element of the instrument optics is the specimen itself.  In order to correct artifacts in the data caused by an evolving apex shape, we are developing a numerical electrostatic model [1, 2] reflecting the physics of both the instrumental pieces as well as the specimen geometry.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, Electrostatic Reconstruction, Numerical Model

title: Introducing a Dynamic Reconstruction Methodology for Multilayered Structures in Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Hatzoglou, Constantinos; Da Costa, Gérald; Wells, Peter; Ren, Xiaochen; Geiser, Brian P.; Larson, David J.; Demoulin, Remi; Hunnestad, Kasper; Talbot, Etienne; Mazumder, Baishakhi; Meier, Dennis; Vurpillot, François
abstract: Atom probe tomography (APT) is a powerful three-dimensional nanoanalyzing microscopy technique considered key in modern materials science. However, progress in the spatial reconstruction of APT data has been rather limited since the first implementation of the protocol proposed by Bas et al. in 1995. This paper proposes a simple semianalytical approach to reconstruct multilayered structures, i.e., two or more different compounds stacked perpendicular to the analysis direction.
keywords: atom probe tomography, dynamic reconstruction, field evaporation simulation, multilayered structure

title: Atom Probe Tomography Measurement of Radiation Enhanced Diffusion
authors: Yano, Kayla H.; Kohnert, Aaron A.; Kaspar, Tiffany C.; Kim, Hyosim; Taylor, Sandra D.; Wang, Yongqiang; Uberuaga, Blas P.; Schreiber, Daniel K.
abstract: Oxidative and corrosive degradation of structural alloys in high-temperature, hostile environments such as nuclear reactors is often mitigated by naturally forming, thin, protective oxide layers that separate the metal from a corrosive media [1]. Cr2O3 (chromia) is a common protective oxide that forms in Ni- and Fe-based alloys and can strongly inhibit ion transport to the underlying metal layer. However, other oxides, such as Fe2O3 (hematite) and Fe3O4 (magnetite), do not effectively inhibit diffusion, corrupting the integrity of the alloy. These generalities apply to high-temperature oxidation, while the behavior under irradiation is far less understood.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, Radiation Enhanced Diffusion, Oxide Layers, Corrosion, High-Temperature Oxidation

title: Silica-embedded Gold Nanoparticles Analyzed by Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Eriksson, Gustav; Hulander, Mats; Thuvander, Mattias; Andersson, Martin
abstract: Nanoparticles are utilized in a multitude of applications due to their unique properties. Consequently, characterization of nanoparticles is crucial, and various methods have been employed in these pursuits. One such method is Atom Probe Tomography (APT). However, existing sample preparation techniques for APT generally involve embedding of the nanoparticles in a matrix different from their environment in solutions or at solid–liquid interfaces. In this work, we demonstrate a methodology based on silica embedding and explore how it can be utilized to form a matrix for nanoparticles suitable for APT analysis.
keywords: atom probe tomography, gold nanoparticles, silica embedding, stabilizing ligands, Liquid-nanoparticle interfaces, Direct imaging

To this day, a fast reconstruction method which utilizes the true potential of APT in terms of resolution does not exist. A model of its effective inverse, the field evaporation, which provides a physically accurate description of the ion trajectories, is a crucial component in reconstruction.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, Simulation, Reconstruction, Field Evaporation

title: Analyzing Linear Features in Atom Probe Tomography Datasets using Skeletonization
authors: Saxena, Alaukik; Kühbach, Markus; Katnagallu, Shyam; Kontis, Paraskevas; Gault, Baptiste; Freysoldt, Christoph
abstract: The ubiquitous defects in crystalline solids play a crucial role in determining their behavior. These defects can be categorized according to their topological dimensionality from 0D, namely point defects such as vacancies, 1D linear defects (dislocations, triple lines), to 2D surfaces, grain boundaries, or stacking faults. Dislocations are essential carriers of plastic deformations and can create or annihilate additional defects when moving, thus mediating the macroscopic mechanical response. Impurities and alloying elements often segregate to these defects, altering the defects’ energetic stability, geometric structure, and mobility. Understanding the interactions between alloying elements and crystal defects is crucial for property engineering. Atom probe tomography (APT) is a characterization routine capable of discerning these interactions.
keywords: Atom probe tomography, dislocations, skeletonization, linear features, material science

title: Advanced Characterization of Metallic Fuels by Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Shah, Sohail; Salvato, Daniele; Kamboj, Anshul; Smith, Charlyne; Yao, Tiankai; Teng, Fei; Giglio, Jeffrey; Bachhav, Mukesh
abstract: Metallic fuels are promising fuel candidates for generation IV advanced reactor concepts and other fast breeder reactor designs. Advancements in metal alloy fuels have focused on developing fuels operating at much higher burnups due to their low smear density. However, numerous studies demonstrate impacts on fuel performance due to fission gas release causing fuel swelling in certain cases. Other phenomena like fuel cladding chemical and mechanical interaction (FCCI and FCMI) influences or limits the performance in different metallic fuels. Additionally, neutron irradiation causes a large number of fission products (FP) to be generated impacting the chemical and mechanical properties of the fuel. These effects are essential to understanding metallic fuels and their irradiation behaviour.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, Metallic Fuels, Fission Gas Release, Fuel Cladding Chemical and Mechanical Interaction, Neutron Irradiation

title: Microstructural Investigation of Porous Plating Copper by Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Kon, Naochika; Nita, Nobuyasu; Uzuhashi, Jun; Ohkubo, Tadakatsu
abstract: In recent years, high speed and larger capacities in data communication have been achieved by increasing the integration of semi-conductor devices. As semiconductor devices become more highly integrated, solder bonding bumps on electronic boards are becoming smaller in diameter and narrower in pitch, and new bonding technologies are required. Conventional solder bonding technology is difficult to miniaturize due to the generation of Kirkendall voids and bridging, as well as the electrical resistance and heat dissipation. Cu-Cu direct bonding is a promising next-generation bonding technology, but Cu pillars alone cannot suppress height variations and defects such as unbonded bumps and voids. Therefore, we aimed to develop a new process to buffer the height variation during bonding by forming a compressible porous layer on the Cu pillars. This study evaluated the microstructure of porous layers to reveal the relationship between the microstructural control and property improvement.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, Porous Plating Copper, Microstructural Investigation

title: Estimation of the Electric Field in Atom Probe Tomography Experiments Using Charge State Ratios
authors: Tegg, Levi; Stephenson, Leigh T.; Cairney, Julie M.
abstract: Kingham [(1982). The post-ionization of field evaporated ions: A theoretical explanation of multiple charge states. Surf Sci 116(2), 273–301] provided equations for the probability of observing higher charge states in atom probe tomography (APT) experiments. These “Kingham curves” have wide application in APT, but cannot be analytically transformed to provide the electric field in terms of the easily measured charge state ratio (CSR). Here we provide a numerical scheme for the calculation of Kingham curves and the variation in electric field with CSR. We find the variation in electric field with CSR is well described by a simple two- or three-parameter equation, and the model is accurate to most elements and charge states. The model is applied to experimental APT data of pure aluminium and a microalloyed steel, demonstrating that the methods described in this work can be easily applied to a variety of APT problems to understand electric field variations.
keywords: Atom probe tomography, Electric field, Charge state ratio, Kingham curves

title: Testing Outlier Detection Algorithms for Identifying Early Stage Solute Clusters in Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Stroud, Ryan S.; Al-Saffar, Ayham; Carter, Megan; Moody, Michael P.; Pedrazzini, Stella; Wenman, Mark R.
abstract: This paper presents a new approach to cluster detection in atom probe tomography (APT) data using the LUNAR and k-NN algorithms. The LUNAR algorithm is a density-based algorithm that uses a graph neural network to identify clusters, while the k-NN algorithm uses a k-nearest neighbors approach to identify clusters. The paper presents a comparison of the performance of the LUNAR and k-NN algorithms with the DBSCAN algorithm, which is a commonly used algorithm for cluster detection in APT data. The results show that the LUNAR and k-NN algorithms outperform the DBSCAN algorithm for cluster sizes above 30. The paper also presents a case study of the application of the LUNAR and k-NN algorithms to a real APT dataset.
keywords: atom probe tomography, clustering, early stage solute clusters, outlier detection, RPV steel, Cluster detection, LUNAR, k-NN, DBSCAN

title: Towards Establishing Best Practice in the Analysis of Hydrogen and Deuterium by Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Gault, Baptiste; Saksena, Aparna; Sauvage, Xavier; Bagot, Paul; Aota, Leonardo S.; Arlt, Jonas; Belkacemi, Lisa T.; Boll, Torben; Chen, Yi-Sheng; Daly, Luke; Djukic, Milos B.; Douglas, James O.; Duarte, Maria J.; Felfer, Peter J.; Forbes, Richard G.; Fu, Jing; Gardner, Hazel M.; Gemma, Ryota; Gerstl, Stephan S. A.; Gong, Yilun; Hachet, Guillaume; Jakob, Severin; Jenkins, Benjamin M.; Jones, Megan E.; Khanchandani, Heena; Kontis, Paraskevas; Krämer, Mathias; Kühbach, Markus; Marceau, Ross K. W.; Mayweg, David; Moore, Katie L.; Nallathambi, Varatharaja; Ott, Benedict C.; Poplawsky, Jonathan D.; Prosa, Ty; Pundt, Astrid; Saha, Mainak; Schwarz, Tim M.; Shang, Yuanyuan; Shen, Xiao; Vrellou, Maria; Yu, Yuan; Zhao, Yujun; Zhao, Huan; Zou, Bowen
abstract: Hydrogen embrittlement of twinning-induced plasticity (TWIP) steels is a critical issue in various industrial applications. In this study, we investigated the contribution of segregation to twin boundaries on the hydrogen embrittlement of TWIP steels using atom probe tomography (APT). Our results show that the segregation of solute atoms to twin boundaries significantly enhances the hydrogen embrittlement of TWIP steels. The segregation of solute atoms to twin boundaries leads to the formation of hydrogen-rich regions, which act as nucleation sites for hydrogen-induced cracking. Our findings provide new insights into the mechanisms of hydrogen embrittlement in TWIP steels and highlight the importance of segregation to twin boundaries in controlling the hydrogen embrittlement of TWIP steels.
keywords: hydrogen, deuterium, atom probe tomography, Hydrogen Analysis, Materials Science, Hydrogen embrittlement, Twinning-induced plasticity steels, Segregation

title: In Situ Focused Ion Beam Redeposition Surface Coatings for Site-Specific, Near-Surface Characterization by Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Guerguis, Bavley; Cuduvally, Ramya; Arcuri, Gabriel; Pourbahari, Bita; Mcdermid, Joseph R.; Pawlowicz, Chris; Langelier, Brian; Bassim, Nabil
abstract: Atom probe tomography (APT) enables three-dimensional chemical mapping with near-atomic scale resolution. However, this method requires precise sample preparation, which is typically achieved using a focused ion beam (FIB) microscope. As the ion beam induces some degree of damage to the sample, it is necessary to apply a protective layer over the region of interest (ROI). Herein, the use of redeposition, a (frequently considered negative) side effect of FIB sputtering, is explored as a technique for targeted surface coatings in site-specific, near-surface APT investigations.
keywords: atom probe tomography, focused ion beam, in situ coating, sample preparation, semiconductor, boron dopant quantification, silicon, electric field strength

title: Mapping the Path to Cryogenic Atom Probe Tomography Analysis of Biomolecules
authors: Woods, Eric V.; Schwarz, Tim M.; Singh, Mahander P.; Zhang, Shuo; Kim, Se-Ho; El-Zoka, Ayman A.; Gremer, Lothar; Willbold, Dieter; Mccarroll, Ingrid; Gault, Baptiste
abstract: To summarize, we have done a systematic study of model amino acids dissolved in pure water by APT. The CIR was introduced as an indicator of local electrostatic field conditions at the time of field evaporation for water-based samples, which appears repeatable across different atom probe systems, however the correlations between experimental conditions are neither direct nor straightforward as many parameters have an influence, to cite a few, the specimen’s length, diameter, composition, the laser pulse energies and repetition rate, or the acquisition mode.
keywords: APT, cryogenic, spectroscopy, tomography, water, Atom Probe Tomography, Amino Acids, Electrostatic Field, Field Evaporation, Cryo-atom probe tomography, Nanoporous copper, Water ice

title: Scanning Transmission Electron Microscopy–Atom Probe Tomography Correlative Analysis for the Characterization of Solute-defect Interactions
authors: Chen, Siwei; Poplawsky, Jonathan D.; Zinkle, Steven J.; Boebinger, Matthew; Zhao, Yajie
abstract: Atom probe tomography (APT) and (scanning) transmission electron microscopy ((S)TEM) are complementary techniques that provide spatially resolved chemical and structural information at the atomic scale.
keywords: atom probe tomography, chemical segregation, correlative analysis, dislocation loop, scanning transmission electron microscopy

title: The Role of Laser Pulse Energy on Data Quality and Reconstruction of Olivine Atom Probe Tomography Data
authors: Dimitriou, Dimitrios; Reddy, Steven M.; Saxey, David W.; Fougerouse, Denis; Evans, Katy; Sun, Xiao; Rickard, William D. A.
abstract: The study of the structure and geochemistry of olivine crystal defects is important but difficult because of their nanometer size and the analytical limitations of most techniques. Laser-assisted atom probe tomography (APT) is capable of sub-nanometer resolution, quantitative geochemical analysis and 3D reconstruction of olivine defects, but optimal analytical conditions and data reconstruction strategies have not been sufficiently studied.
keywords: atom probe tomography, electric field, laser pulse energy, olivine, spatial reconstruction, common minerals

title: Terahertz Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Vella, Angela; Houard, Jonathan; De Tullio, Matteo; Karam, Michella; Blum, Ivan; Ropitaux, Marc; Andersson, Martin; Eriksson, Gustav; Hideur, Ammar
abstract: The use of ultrashort phenomena to trigger the field evaporation of the ion is mandatory in atomic probe tomography to perform time-of-flight mass spectrometry. Today, either laser or electric pulses are used depending on the material being analyzed, but their disadvantages are well known: laser pulses heat up the sample [1], while electric pulses produce ions with a large energy spread [2]. In this talk, we propose a newly developed instrument that uses a high-amplitude terahertz (THz) monocycle to trigger field evaporation. We will present the configuration based on two-color filamentation in air coupled with a custom APT, as shown in Figure 1. First results on metals show that there is neither ions energy spread nor sample temperature increase. The THz monocycle acts as an ultra-short electrical pulse [3-5]. Further tests have also been performed on pure silicon nanotips, where the thermal effects are visible and strongly dependent on the spectrum of the THz radiation. Finally, we analyzed nanotips made of sol-gel amorphous silica, which is chosen as matrix for bio-molecules embedding for future studies [6,8].
keywords: Terahertz, Atom Probe Tomography, Field Evaporation, Time-of-Flight Mass Spectrometry

title: Microstructural Analysis of TaAlN Nitride MAX Phases by Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Knipling, Keith; Katzer, D. Scott; Lock, Evgeniya
abstract: Nitride MAX phases are a promising class of functional materials for extreme-temperature applications due to their unique combination of thermal stability, oxidation resistance, and mechanical strength at high temperatures.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, MAX Phases, TaAlN, Nitride

title: Irradiation Effects in Zirconium Nuclear Fuel Cladding Tubes Investigated by Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Mayweg, David; Eriksson, Johan; Andrén, Hans-Olof; Thuvander, Mattias
abstract: In a light water nuclear reactor, uranium dioxide fuel pellets are contained inside long thin-walled tubes made of zirconium (Zr) alloys. The main reason for using Zr-alloys is the very low cross-section of Zr for thermal neutrons. In addition, they exhibit good resistance to water corrosion [1]. The neutron exposure results in significant irradiation damage during the lifetime (a few years) of the cladding tubes [2], with fluences reaching up to a few tens of dpa (displacements per atom). This results in irradiation growth and creep [3]. In many cases, the corrosion rate increases after a few years, a phenomenon called break-away corrosion, which also results in an increase in hydrogen pick-up and hydride formation [4]. In this work, cladding tubes from boiling water reactors have been studied using atom probe tomography (APT), a technique that can reveal microstructural changes on the nanometer level, with the aim of understanding the behavior of the various alloying elements and their impact on break-away corrosion.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, Zirconium Nuclear Fuel Cladding Tubes, Irradiation Effects, Break-Away Corrosion

title: Advancing Atom Probe Tomography of SrTiO3: Measurement Methodology and Impurity Detection Limits
authors: Rybak, Janina Malin; Arlt, Jonas; Gault, Baptiste; Volkert, Cynthia A.
abstract: This study establishes a robust APT methodology for nanoscale chemical analysis of strontium titanate. By applying thin metal coatings to STO specimens, high-yield APT measurements become feasible, since the coatings shield the material from strong electric fields and reduce field-induced mechanical stress and fracture.
keywords: atom probe tomography, detection limit, specimen fracture, strontium titanate, Nanoscale Chemical Analysis

title: Look What You Made Me Glue: SEMGlu™ Enabled Alternative Cryogenic Sample Preparation Process for Cryogenic Atom Probe Tomography Studies
authors: Mulcahy, Neil; Douglas, James O.; Conroy, Michele Shelly
abstract: Extensive efforts have been applied to develop workflows for sample preparation of specimens for atom probe tomography at cryogenic temperatures. This is primarily due to the difficulty involved in preparing site-specific lift-out samples at cryogenic temperatures without the assistance of the gas injection system (GIS) as using it under cryogenic conditions leads to nonuniform and difficult to control deposition.
keywords: atom probe tomography, cryogenic, focused ion beam, sample preparation, SEMGlu

title: Atom Probe Tomography of Hydrated Biomacromolecules: Preliminary Results
authors: Zhang, Shuo; Aota, Leonardo Shoji; Singh, Mahander P.; Woods, Eric V.; Périer Jouet, Fantine; Schwarz, Tim M.; Gault, Baptiste
abstract: This paper presents preliminary results on the analysis of hydrated biological specimens using atom probe tomography (APT). The specimens were prepared using a cryo-focused ion beam (FIB) and analyzed using a LEAP 3000X HR instrument. The results show that the APT technique can be used to analyze hydrated biological specimens, but the analysis is challenging due to the complexity of the specimens and the limitations of the APT technique. The paper discusses the challenges and limitations of APT analysis of hydrated biological specimens and presents some preliminary results on the analysis of ferritin particles.
keywords: atom probe tomography, biomacromolecules, cryogenic, ferritin, hydrated biological specimens, ferritin particles, cryo-focused ion beam, LEAP 3000X HR, Serial lift-out, cryo-EM, molecular anatomy, whole organisms

title: APT Calibrator: A Multifunctional C# Software Application for Reconstruction Calibration in Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Chen, Xinren; Li, Yue; Woods, Eric; Zhou, Xuyang; Gault, Baptiste
abstract: In this work, we developed APT Calibrator, a user-friendly software suite with visualization tools that streamlines key calibration steps in atom probe tomography. Its core features include image compression factor (ICF) calibration, field factor (kf) calibration, pole-figure simulation and indexing, and a correlative dynamic reconstruction workflow.
keywords: atom probe tomography, crystallographic pole figure, dynamic reconstruction, image compression factor, Correlative reconstruction, Golden seeds

title: Overcoming Challenges in Atom Probe Tomography of Carbonate Minerals: Application of In Situ Chromium Coatings for Improved Experiment Yield
authors: Dubosq, Renelle; Schwarz, Tim M.; Saksena, Aparna; Bakowsky, Christina; Gault, Baptiste
abstract: Carbonate minerals such as calcite cover a significant portion of Earth’s ice-free land surface. Beyond their widespread distribution, they play a critical role in geological processes, including the global carbon cycle and various biogeochemical processes. Understanding the crystal chemistry of carbonates is therefore essential for advancing our knowledge of these systems. Atom probe tomography offers promising potential for revealing nanoscale chemical and isotopic processes in minerals; however, its application to carbonates remains technically challenging due to their poor thermal conductivity and absorption. In this study, we apply and adapt an in situ metallic coating technique and optimize atom probe tomography acquisition parameters for calcite. The results demonstrate that applying a Cr coating to calcite specimens significantly improves experimental yield and enhances mass resolution during atom probe analysis. Despite these improvements, the data do not yield stoichiometric proportions for calcite due to the post-ionization dissociation of CxOy molecules into neutrals.
keywords: atom probe tomography, calcite, chromium coating, mineral chemistry, Bone microstructure, Near-atomic scale

title: Atom Probe Tomography / Atomsondentomographie
authors: Felfer, P.; Stevenson, L.; Li, T.
abstract: In the past two decades, atom probe tomography (APT) has become a method that is making significant impact on the field of materials science and recently, also beyond [1]. It is a combination of a microscope and a mass spectrometer, able to provide 3D data from a wide variety of solids at the atomic scale.
keywords: Atom Probe Tomography, Materials Science, Field Evaporation

abstract: We investigate the mechanisms of a laser-assisted atom probe tomography technique. In this method, a sub-wavelength tip is subjected both to a very strong static electric field and to a femtosecond laser pulse. As a result, ions are ejected from the tip one by one. By using femtosecond lasers, one can analyze not only metals but also semiconductors and dielectric materials.

title: Integrated textural and geochemical analysis of igneous zircon by atom probe tomography

abstract: Mechanisms relating to growth and/or compositional modification of zircon occur at the atomic scale. For felsic igneous systems, processes responsible for growth patterns in zircon have previously remained elusive as the volume of material needed to analyze these compositional features using traditional in-situ methods is considerably larger than the typical sub-micron scale distribution of trace elements.

We introduce a new method to determine nanoscale precipitate number densities of macroscopic samples that is based on the matrix dissolution technique (MDT) and combine it with atom probe tomography (APT).

title: Interpreting nanovoids in atom probe tomography data for accurate local compositional measurements
authors: Wang, Xing; Hatzoglou, Constantinos; Sneed, Brian; Fan, Zhe; Guo, Wei; Jin, Ke; Chen, Di; Bei, Hongbin; Wang, Yongqiang; Weber, William J.; Zhang, Yanwen; Gault, Baptiste; More, Karren L.; Vurpillot, Francois; Poplawsky, Jonathan D.
abstract: Quantifying chemical compositions around nanovoids is a fundamental task for research and development of various materials. Atom probe tomography (APT) and scanning transmission electron microscopy (STEM) are currently the most suitable tools because of their ability to probe materials at the nanoscale. Both techniques have limitations, particularly APT, because of insufficient understanding of void imaging.

title: Quantifying short-range order using atom probe tomography
authors: He, Mengwei; Davids, William J.; Breen, Andrew J.; Ringer, Simon P.
abstract: The characterization of short-range order (SRO) in multicomponent high-entropy alloys (HEAs) is crucial for understanding their unique properties and behavior. In this study, we present a comprehensive approach for the characterization of SRO in HEAs using atom probe tomography (APT). We demonstrate the ability to detect and quantify SRO in HEAs with high accuracy and precision, and show that the SRO parameter can be used to predict the mechanical properties of HEAs. Our results highlight the importance of SRO in understanding the behavior of HEAs and demonstrate the potential of APT for characterizing SRO in these complex materials.
keywords: Atom probe tomography, short-range order, medium-entropy alloys, high-entropy alloys, Mechanical Properties

title: Understanding the Effects of Tetrahedral Site Occupancy by the Zn Dopant in Li-NMCs toward High-Voltage Compositional−Structural−Mechanical Stability via Operando and 3D Atom Probe Tomography Studies
authors: Sharma, Ankur; Pandey, Adityanarayan H.; Jangid, Manoj K.; Srihari, Velaga; Poswal, Himanshu K.; Mukhopadhyay, Amartya
abstract: Continuing from our previously reported work,30 where Zn-doping at the tetrahedral site of the Li-layer in the Li-NMC structure was found to nearly completely suppress the Ni-migration to the Li-layer even upon deep delithiation, leading to outstanding improvement in the high voltage cyclic stability, the present work provides further insights and understandings into the beneficial effects of such doping, from the structural, mechanical, and compositional point of views.
